title: Activation Recomputation, Gradient Accumulation, and Data Parallelism
description: Three foundational techniques that reduce memory and scale training: recomputing activations, accumulating gradients, and replicating your model across GPUs.
tags: Gradient Accumulation: Big Batches Without Big Memory
Data Parallelism Optimizations: Overlap, Bucketing, and Scaling

notebooks.title: Activation Checkpointing
notebooks.objective: Understand why activations dominate GPU memory, implement activation checkpointing from scratch, use PyTorch's built-in checkpoint API, measure memory savings and compute overhead, and compare full vs selective checkpointing.

notebooks.title: Gradient Accumulation
notebooks.objective: Prove the mathematical equivalence between large-batch and accumulated micro-batch gradients, implement gradient accumulation in a clean training loop, identify common pitfalls, and combine accumulation with activation checkpointing.

notebooks.title: Data Parallelism
notebooks.objective: Simulate multi-GPU data parallelism on a single GPU, implement manual AllReduce gradient synchronization, verify that data parallel training matches single-GPU results, analyze communication overhead and scaling efficiency, and combine all three memory techniques.

curator.name: Dr. Rajat Dandekar
curator.title: Course Instructor
curator.bio: Dr. Rajat Dandekar is a researcher and educator specializing in AI/ML, with a passion for making complex concepts accessible through intuitive explanations and hands-on learning.
